title: chrysanthemum indicum var. procumbens
intro: Chrysanthemum indicum var. procumbens, commonly known as procumbent chrysanthemum, is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family and is traditionally used in herbal medicine. This plant has been found to offer several health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation and the improvement of cardiovascular health. The therapeutic actions of procumbent chrysanthemum include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its ability to modulate the immune system and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to contribute to its medicinal properties.
benefits: Chrysanthemum indicum var. procumbens has many benefits, such as being a natural anti-inflammatory agent, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce swelling, and ease pain. Its antioxidant properties can protect the body from cell damage, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce the risk of certain cancers, including breast, prostate, and colon cancer. Additionally, this plant has been found to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can aid in wound healing, prevent infections, and even help manage digestive issues such as diarrhea and constipation. By incorporating Chrysanthemum indicum var. procumbens into one's daily routine, individuals can experience improved overall health, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, ultimately leading to a longer and healthier life.
actions: Chrysanthemum indicum var. procumbens has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, allowing individuals to enjoy better sleep quality and wake up feeling refreshed. Its analgesic and antipyretic properties can provide relief from headaches and fever, making it easier for people to focus on their daily tasks and maintain their productivity. Additionally, its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties can help protect against oxidative stress and infections, promoting overall health and well-being and enabling individuals to engage in physical activities with confidence. By harnessing the therapeutic actions of Chrysanthemum indicum var. procumbens, people can experience improved physical and mental health, leading to enhanced quality of life and a greater sense of satisfaction.
intro_study: According to "Plants (Basel, Switzerland)", chrysanthemum indicum var. procumbens benefits from auxin treatment, particularly with indole-3-butyric acid (IBA), which effectively induces roots and promotes the synthesis and accumulation of phenolic compounds.
constituents: Chrysanthemum indicum var. procumbens has many active constituents, such as flavonoids (e.g., quercetin, kaempferol), triterpenoids (e.g., ursolic acid, oleanolic acid), and sesquiterpenes (e.g., eupatoriopicrin, costunolide), as well as volatile oils (e.g., borneol, linalool), and alkaloids (e.g., chrysoplenol). These compounds have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, and have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can improve people's daily lives by reducing the risk of chronic diseases, such as heart disease and cancer, and alleviating symptoms of allergies and colds. The flavonoids in Chrysanthemum indicum var. procumbens have been found to have cardiovascular benefits by lowering blood pressure and improving blood lipid profiles, while the triterpenoids have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects by protecting the liver from damage. Overall, the diverse array of medicinal constituents in Chrysanthemum indicum var. procumbens makes it a valuable plant for promoting health and well-being.
parts: Chrysanthemum indicum var. procumbens has many medicinal parts, such as the flowers, which are used to treat fever, headache, and menstrual disorders due to their content of flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol. The roots of this plant are also utilized in traditional medicine to treat rheumatism and arthritis due to their high content of sesquiterpenoids like sesquiterpene lactones. The leaves and stems of Chrysanthemum indicum var. procumbens are used to treat inflammatory conditions and gastrointestinal disorders due to their content of phenolic acids like caffeic acid and ferulic acid. Additionally, the plant's essential oil, which is extracted from its flowers and leaves, contains compounds like limonene and beta-pinene, which are used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
preparations: Chrysanthemum indicum var. procumbens has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, that have been used for medicinal purposes. Chrysanthemum tea, for instance, is often used to lower blood pressure and reduce fever, while tinctures can be used to treat anxiety and insomnia due to their sedative properties. Infusions of the plant have also been used to soothe digestive issues and alleviate headaches. Additionally, dried flowers of Chrysanthemum indicum var. procumbens can be made into a powder for topical use, which is said to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
side_effects: Chrysanthemum indicum var. procumbens can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues in some individuals. Excessive consumption of the plant's extracts may lead to dizziness, headaches, and nausea. In rare cases, using the plant for extended periods or in high doses can cause liver damage and interact with certain medications. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Chrysanthemum indicum var. procumbens for medicinal purposes.
